biopsyNotes:
A. Shave Removal
Clinical Diagnosis: C005 Aesthetics Blemishes
Location: Left Upper Back
Method: Shave
Lesion Size: 
The patient was evaluated preoperative and noted to be in stable condition. 
 Informed consent was obtained from the patient. All risks, benefits, and alternatives regarding the survey have been reviewed in detail with the patient. 
 This includes risks of bleeding, infection, scarring recurrence of lesion and need for further procedures. 
After consent was obtained, attention was turned toward the area of interest which was disinfected and cleaned with alcohol. Local anesthetic, 1% lidocaine with epinephrine was infiltrated around the site. An elliptical incision with the appropriate margins was made using a Number 15. Dissection of the surrounding/overlying tissue was performed to isolate, expose and remove the lesion. The specimen was placed in a bottle labeled A. #RA19-1671. 
The patient tolerated the procedure well with no complications and minimal bleeding.
 bacitracin was applied at the wound site and covered with a press dressing. Wound care instructions reviewed and given to the patient.
Repair: 
Pressure was applied to achieve hemostasis. Bacitracin was applied at the wound site and it was bandaged. Patient tolerated the procedure well and wound care instructions were reviewed and given to the patient.

biopsyNotes:
B. Shave Removal
Clinical Diagnosis: L91.0 Scar Keloid
Location: Umbilicus
Method: Shave
Lesion Size: 4.0 - 4.0 cm
Excision Size, including margins: 4.0 x 4 cm
The patient was evaluated preoperative and noted to be in stable condition. 
 Informed consent was obtained from the patient. All risks, benefits, and alternatives regarding the survey have been reviewed in detail with the patient. 
 This includes risks of bleeding, infection, scarring recurrence of lesion and need for further procedures. 
After consent was obtained, attention was turned toward the area of interest which was disinfected and cleaned with alcohol. Local anesthetic, 2% lidocaine with epinephrine was infiltrated around the site. An elliptical incision with the appropriate margins was made using a Number 15. Dissection of the surrounding/overlying tissue was performed to isolate, expose and remove the lesion. The specimen was placed in a bottle labeled B. #WC24-1746. 
The patient tolerated the procedure well with no complications and minimal bleeding.
 Vaseline was applied at the wound site and covered with a press dressing. Wound care instructions reviewed and given to the patient.
Repair: Electrodessication 
The wound was left to heal by secondary intention after electrodessication for homeostasis. 
Pressure was applied to achieve hemostasis. Vaseline was applied at the wound site and it was bandaged. Patient tolerated the procedure well and wound care instructions were reviewed and given to the patient.
